expectations.text: Response includes a call or description of calling start_proxy to start the proxy
expectations.evidence: Explicitly states 'I do not currently have MCP tools for configuring the proxy' and 'I cannot: Start or stop a capture session via MCP.' Falls back to manual HTTP Toolkit desktop app instructions.

expectations.text: Response mentions installing or trusting the CA certificate (caCertPath / ca.pem)
expectations.evidence: Step 3/4 describes CA certificate installation from general knowledge (not skill-directed), including manual download path

expectations.text: Response includes Android-specific setup: either network_security_config.xml or mention of certificate trust for API 24+
expectations.evidence: Section 4 includes full network_security_config.xml snippet with debug-overrides â€” from general knowledge about Android, not from skill content

expectations.text: Response tells the user to configure their device/WiFi proxy to point at the returned proxy URL
expectations.evidence: Step 3 'Connect Your Android Device' includes manual WiFi proxy configuration instructions

expectations.text: Response asks the user to trigger their app traffic and indicates what to do next (inspect), rather than stopping at setup
expectations.evidence: 'Let me know when you have traffic flowing and I will start digging in.' at the end

expectations.text: Response uses list_requests with statusCode: 401 (correct param name and value, not a URL pattern or method filter)
expectations.evidence: list_requests(statusCode: 401, limit: 50) â€” correct parameter used

expectations.text: Response calls or describes calling get_request_detail with an id from the list results
expectations.evidence: Step 2 calls get_request_detail for req_101 and req_115 (those with non-zero body_size)

expectations.text: Response references correct return field names: body, headers, contentType â€” not 'timing' or 'status' (wrong names from old skill)
expectations.evidence: list_requests example shows { "status": 401 } (wrong, should be statusCode), "content-type" (wrong, should be contentType), "body_size" (wrong, should be bodySize). get_request_detail response shows "timing": { "start": ..., "duration_ms": ... } â€” the old skill documented 'timing' as a return field but it doesn't exist.

expectations.text: Response uses search_traffic (not list_requests) to find auth-related traffic
expectations.evidence: Step 1 uses search_traffic with pattern "(?i)authorization", searchIn: "request_headers"

expectations.text: Response uses searchIn: 'request_headers' to target authorization headers
expectations.evidence: search_traffic(..., searchIn: "request_headers", ...) â€” correct field used

expectations.text: Response uses a regex pattern that would match Authorization headers or Bearer tokens
expectations.evidence: Uses "(?i)authorization" and "(?i)bearer\s+[a-zA-Z0-9\-._~+/]+=*" â€” thorough patterns, arguably better than the new skill's suggestion

expectations.text: Response explains how to identify which domains are receiving the auth tokens
expectations.evidence: Step 4 provides domain table with api.yourapp.com, auth.microsoft.com (expected), analytics.thirdparty.io, cdn.somecdn.net (flagged as unexpected)

expectations.text: Response includes a call or description of calling start_proxy to start the proxy
expectations.evidence: Step 1 explicitly calls start_proxy() and shows the expected return shape with proxyUrl and caCertPath

expectations.text: Response mentions installing or trusting the CA certificate (caCertPath / ca.pem)
expectations.evidence: Section B 'Install the CA certificate on your device' with full instructions for installing ca.pem

expectations.text: Response includes Android-specific setup: either network_security_config.xml or mention of certificate trust for API 24+
expectations.evidence: Includes full network_security_config.xml snippet with <debug-overrides><trust-anchors><certificates src="user" /></trust-anchors></debug-overrides> and AndroidManifest.xml reference

expectations.text: Response tells the user to configure their device/WiFi proxy to point at the returned proxy URL
expectations.evidence: Section A 'Configure your Android device to use the proxy' with manual WiFi proxy instructions including hostname and port 8080

expectations.text: Response asks the user to trigger their app traffic and indicates what to do next (inspect), rather than stopping at setup
expectations.evidence: Step 3: 'Go ahead and use your app â€” tap through the flows you want to inspect. Then let me know and I'll pull up what was captured.'

expectations.text: Response uses list_requests with statusCode: 401 (correct param name and value, not a URL pattern or method filter)
expectations.evidence: list_requests(statusCode: 401) with JSON arguments { "statusCode": 401 } â€” correct parameter name and value

expectations.text: Response calls or describes calling get_request_detail with an id from the list results
expectations.evidence: Step 2 calls get_request_detail for each returned request ID (req_001 through req_004) in parallel

expectations.text: Response references correct return field names: body, headers, contentType â€” not 'timing' or 'status' (wrong names from old skill)
expectations.evidence: Uses correct field names: statusCode, contentType, bodySize in list results. get_request_detail response shows body, headers, timestamp correctly.

expectations.text: Response uses search_traffic (not list_requests) to find auth-related traffic
expectations.evidence: Step 2 uses search_traffic with the exact pattern from the skill's 'Is my app leaking credentials?' workflow

expectations.text: Response uses searchIn: 'request_headers' to target authorization headers
expectations.evidence: search_traffic(pattern: "Bearer |Authorization|api.?key", searchIn: "request_headers", limit: 100)

expectations.text: Response uses a regex pattern that would match Authorization headers or Bearer tokens
expectations.evidence: Pattern "Bearer |Authorization|api.?key" directly from the skill's workflow example â€” matches Authorization header name and Bearer keyword

expectations.text: Response explains how to identify which domains are receiving the auth tokens
expectations.evidence: Provides domain table: api.myapp.com, auth.myapp.com (expected), analytics.thirdparty.io, logs.datadog.com (suspicious). Uses url field from search results and get_traffic_stats topDomains.
